title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rcloner provides R interface rclone, command-line program supports 40 cloud storage backends, including: S3-compatible stores: Amazon S3, MinIO, Ceph, Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2, … Google Cloud Storage Google Drive Azure Blob Storage Dropbox, OneDrive, Box, many others file operations (copy, sync, list, move, delete, …) use consistent interface regardless storage backend.

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: Install CRAN: development version GitHub:

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rcloner automatically locates system-installed rclone binary. rclone already PATH, install : downloads appropriate pre-built binary https://downloads.rclone.org operating system architecture stores user-writable directory — system privileges required. Check installed version:

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rcloner manages cloud storage credentials remotes, named configurations stored rclone’s config file.

title: Getting started with rcloner
code: rclone_config_create(   "aws",   type     = "s3",   provider = "AWS",   access_key_id     = Sys.getenv("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"),   region            = "us-east-1" )

title: Getting started with rcloner
code: rclone_config_create(   "minio",   type     = "s3",   provider = "Minio",   access_key_id     = Sys.getenv("MINIO_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("MINIO_SECRET_KEY"),   endpoint          = "https://minio.example.com" )

title: Getting started with rcloner
Getting started with rcloner
text: rclone_ls() returns data frame objects given path.

title: Getting started with rcloner
code: # List a local directory rclone_ls(tempdir(), files_only = TRUE)

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rclone_ls() parses rclone lsjson output returns data frame columns Path, Name, Size, MimeType, ModTime, IsDir.
code: # List a bucket on a configured remote rclone_ls("aws:my-bucket")  # Recursive listing rclone_ls("aws:my-bucket/data/", recursive = TRUE)  # Directories only rclone_lsd("aws:my-bucket")

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rclone_copy() copies files source destination, skipping identical files. never deletes destination files.
code: src  <- tempfile() dest <- tempfile() dir.create(src) dir.create(dest) writeLines("hello from rcloner", file.path(src, "readme.txt"))  rclone_copy(src, dest) list.files(dest)

title: Getting started with rcloner
code: # Upload a local directory to S3 rclone_copy("/local/data", "aws:my-bucket/data")  # Download a file from S3 rclone_copy("aws:my-bucket/report.csv", "/local/downloads/")  # Copy a URL directly to cloud storage (no local intermediate) rclone_copyurl(   "https://example.com/dataset.csv",   "aws:my-bucket/dataset.csv" )

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rclone_sync() makes destination identical source, deleting destination files source. Use care.

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: rclone_move() copies files deletes source.

title: Getting started with rcloner
code: # Read a remote file into R contents <- rclone_cat("aws:my-bucket/config.yaml")  # Get metadata for an object rclone_stat("aws:my-bucket/data.csv")  # Total size of a path rclone_size("aws:my-bucket")  # Create a bucket/directory rclone_mkdir("aws:new-bucket")  # Delete files (keeps directories) rclone_delete("aws:my-bucket/old-data/")  # Remove a path and all its contents rclone_purge("aws:my-bucket/scratch")  # Generate a public link (where supported) rclone_link("aws:my-bucket/report.html")  # Get storage quota info rclone_about("aws:")

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: Every rclone subcommand accessible via rclone() function, accepts character vector arguments:
code: # Equivalent to: rclone version rclone("version")  # Run any rclone command rclone(c("check", "aws:bucket", "/local/backup", "--one-way"))

title: Getting started with rcloner
text: migrating minioclient package, function mapping : main difference rcloner uses remotes (e.g. "aws:bucket") rather aliases (e.g. "alias/bucket"). Remote configuration done rclone_config_create() instead mc_alias_set().

title: Authors and Citation
text: Carl Boettiger. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Boettiger C (2026). rcloner: Interface 'rclone' Cloud Storage Utility. R package version 0.0.1, https://boettiger-lab.github.io/rcloner/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rcloner: Interface to 'rclone' Cloud Storage Utility},   author = {Carl Boettiger},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://boettiger-lab.github.io/rcloner/}, }

what: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: rcloner provides R interface rclone, battle-tested, open-source command-line program managing files cloud storage. rclone supports 40 cloud storage providers — Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, Azure Blob Storage, MinIO, Ceph, Dropbox, many others — single consistent interface.

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: One package clouds. rclone’s backend system means can use R functions regardless storage provider. Actively maintained upstream. rclone large, vibrant open-source community updated frequently. credentials R. rclone handles authentication securely; rcloner simply calls binary. Familiar output. Listing functions return standard R data frames.

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: Development version:

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: rclone already system, install within R: downloads appropriate pre-built binary https://downloads.rclone.org platform. system privileges required.

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
code: library(rcloner) rclone_version() #> rclone v1.60.1-DEV #> - os/version: ubuntu 24.04 (64 bit) #> - os/kernel: 6.14.0-32-generic (x86_64) #> - os/type: linux #> - os/arch: amd64 #> - go/version: go1.22.2 #> - go/linking: dynamic #> - go/tags: none

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
code: # Amazon S3 rclone_config_create(   "aws",   type              = "s3",   provider          = "AWS",   access_key_id     = Sys.getenv("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"),   region            = "us-east-1" )  # MinIO / S3-compatible rclone_config_create(   "minio",   type              = "s3",   provider          = "Minio",   access_key_id     = Sys.getenv("MINIO_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("MINIO_SECRET_KEY"),   endpoint          = "https://minio.example.com" )

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
code: # Works on local paths too — no cloud account needed rclone_ls(tempdir(), files_only = TRUE) #>                       Path                     Name   Size #> 1 callr-env-2819ce52fd8700 callr-env-2819ce52fd8700 262298 #>                   MimeType                             ModTime IsDir #> 1 application/octet-stream 2026-03-16T14:32:59.514146342-07:00 FALSE # Cloud path rclone_ls("aws:my-bucket", recursive = TRUE)

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
code: src  <- tempfile() dest <- tempfile() dir.create(src);  dir.create(dest) writeLines("hello from rcloner", file.path(src, "readme.txt"))  rclone_copy(src, dest) list.files(dest) #> [1] "readme.txt" # Upload to S3 rclone_copy("/local/data", "aws:my-bucket/data")  # Sync (deletes destination files not in source) rclone_sync("aws:my-bucket", "/local/backup")  # Copy a URL directly to cloud storage rclone_copyurl("https://example.com/data.csv", "aws:my-bucket/data.csv")

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
code: rclone_cat("aws:my-bucket/README.txt")   # read a file rclone_stat("aws:my-bucket/data.csv")   # file metadata rclone_size("aws:my-bucket")            # total size rclone_mkdir("aws:new-bucket")          # create bucket rclone_delete("aws:my-bucket/old/")     # delete files rclone_purge("aws:scratch")             # remove path + contents rclone_link("aws:my-bucket/report.html")# public link rclone_about("aws:")                    # quota info

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: rcloner uses remotes (e.g. "aws:bucket") rather aliases (e.g. "myalias/bucket"). Configure remotes rclone_config_create() instead mc_alias_set().

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: rcloner R wrapper around rclone, open-source cloud storage utility written Go maintained rclone community. cloud storage work performed rclone binary; package simply provides convenient R interface . See rclone documentation full list supported backends configuration options.

title: Interface to rclone Cloud Storage Utility
text: MIT © Carl Boettiger. rclone licensed MIT License.

title: Install the rclone binary — install_rclone
text: Downloads installs rclone binary current platform. binary stored user-writable directory require system-level installation.

title: Install the rclone binary — install_rclone
code: install_rclone(   force = FALSE,   dest = getOption("rcloner.dir", tools::R_user_dir("rcloner", "data")) )

title: Install the rclone binary — install_rclone
text: force Logical; TRUE, re-download even rclone already installed. Default FALSE. dest Directory install rclone. Defaults value options("rcloner.dir"), falling back tools::R_user_dir("rcloner", "data").

title: Install the rclone binary — install_rclone
text: Invisibly, path installed rclone binary.

title: Install the rclone binary — install_rclone
Run an rclone command — rclone
text: Low-level wrapper runs rclone command. Higher-level functions like rclone_copy(), rclone_ls(), etc. call internally.

title: Run an rclone command — rclone
code: rclone(args = character(), env = NULL, echo = FALSE, error_on_status = TRUE)

title: Run an rclone command — rclone
text: args Character vector arguments passed rclone (command flags). Alternatively single space-delimited string. env Named character vector environment variables, NULL. echo Logical; TRUE, print stdout/stderr produced. Default FALSE. error_on_status Logical; TRUE (default), stop error rclone exits non-zero status.

title: Run an rclone command — rclone
text: list elements status, stdout, stderr, timeout (invisibly).

title: Run an rclone command — rclone

title: Output file content to R — rclone_cat

what: Check whether two remotes are in sync — rclone_check
title: Check whether two remotes are in sync — rclone_check
text: Compares files src dest without transferring anything. Differences reported messages.

title: Check whether two remotes are in sync — rclone_check
Check whether two remotes are in sync — rclone_check
text: src Source path. dest Destination path. ... Additional rclone flags.

title: Check whether two remotes are in sync — rclone_check
text: Invisibly, rclone result list (check stderr differences).

title: Configure an rclone remote — rclone_config_create
text: Creates updates named remote rclone config file. primary way register cloud storage credentials rclone.

title: Configure an rclone remote — rclone_config_create
Configure an rclone remote — rclone_config_create
text: name Name remote (used prefix paths, e.g. "myremote:bucket/key"). type Remote type, e.g. "s3", "gcs", "azureblob", "drive". Run rclone("help backends") see supported types. ... Named parameters passed remote configuration. S3 remotes typically includes provider, access_key_id, secret_access_key, optionally endpoint (non-AWS S3).

title: Configure an rclone remote — rclone_config_create
text: Invisibly, result rclone command.

title: Configure an rclone remote — rclone_config_create
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # AWS S3 rclone_config_create("aws", type = "s3",   provider = "AWS",   access_key_id = Sys.getenv("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"),   region = "us-east-1" )  # MinIO / S3-compatible rclone_config_create("minio", type = "s3",   provider = "Minio",   access_key_id = Sys.getenv("MINIO_ACCESS_KEY"),   secret_access_key = Sys.getenv("MINIO_SECRET_KEY"),   endpoint = "https://minio.example.com" ) } # }

what: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls
title: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls
text: Returns data frame objects given path, parsed rclone's JSON output. primary listing function maps rclone lsjson.

title: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls
code: rclone_ls(path, recursive = FALSE, dirs_only = FALSE, files_only = FALSE, ...)

title: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls
text: path Remote path rclone syntax, e.g. "myremote:bucket/prefix". local paths use filesystem path directly. recursive Logical; TRUE, list objects recursively. Default FALSE. dirs_only Logical; TRUE, list directories. Default FALSE. files_only Logical; TRUE, list files (directories). Default FALSE. ... Additional flags passed rclone lsjson strings (e.g. "--fast-list").

title: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls
text: data frame columns Path, Name, Size, MimeType, ModTime, IsDir, optionally others returned rclone.

title: List objects in a remote path — rclone_ls

List directories in a remote path — rclone_lsd

title: List directories in a remote path — rclone_lsd
text: data frame (see rclone_ls()).

title: rcloner 0.0.1
text: Initial release providing R interface ‘rclone’ command-line utility. install_rclone() downloads appropriate rclone binary current platform. rclone() provides low-level interface rclone subcommand. High-level wrappers: rclone_ls(), rclone_copy(), rclone_sync(), rclone_move(), rclone_cat(), rclone_mkdir(), rclone_rmdir(), rclone_delete(), rclone_purge(), rclone_stat(), rclone_size(), rclone_check(), rclone_link(), rclone_about(), rclone_version(). Configuration helpers: rclone_config_create(), rclone_config_set(), rclone_config_show(), rclone_config_delete(), rclone_listremotes().
